selfEvalCaveat: This report was produced by the same agent that wrote the code. Per the harness's structural-separation principle, an independent evaluator with a fresh context should re-verify these results. The sprint-02 precedent shows this pattern: the original self-eval was later superseded by a fresh-context independent eval. All evidence below is reproducible from the current commit.

evidence.pytest: 44 passed in 0.45s (21 sprint-02 + 23 sprint-01 contract-mapped tests)
evidence.headlessRun: python -m whisperdeep --headless --seed 1 -> exit 0; 40 lines x 81 chars; '@' count = 1; '#' count >= 1; stderr empty (0 bytes)
evidence.reproducibility: two --seed 1 --headless runs produced byte-identical stdout (diff -q reports identical)
evidence.help: python -m whisperdeep --help -> exit 0; argparse usage shown
evidence.directApi: wall.glyph='#'/walkable=False, floor.glyph='.'/walkable=True; Floor(20,10) reports w=20 h=10 and in_bounds(-1,0)=False; Player(5,7) -> x=5 y=7 glyph='@' subclass of Entity; Game.from_seed(1) places player at (22,35) in-bounds; render_floor(Floor(10,5)) returns 5 lines of width 10; render_frame contains '@' exactly once; unique frame glyphs subset of {#,.,+,>,<,@}

criteria.description: whisperdeep package importable
criteria.evidence: tests/test_sprint01.py::test_c1_package_importable PASSED. `python -c 'import whisperdeep'` exits 0. whisperdeep/__init__.py exposes __version__='0.2.0'.

criteria.description: python -m whisperdeep runnable; --help exits 0
criteria.evidence: tests/test_sprint01.py::test_c2_main_module_help_exits_zero PASSED. Manual: `python -m whisperdeep --help` prints argparse usage and exits 0. whisperdeep/__main__.py imports cli.main and sys.exit()s on its return.

criteria.description: Distinct modules: tiles, floor, entity, game, render
criteria.evidence: tests/test_sprint01.py::test_c3_modules_all_exist_and_import PASSED. Files present: whisperdeep/tiles.py, floor.py, entity.py, game.py, render.py. All five import cleanly.

criteria.description: Tile abstraction with WALL/FLOOR; glyphs '#'/'.'; walkable flags
criteria.evidence: tests/test_sprint01.py::test_c4_wall_tile_glyph_and_walkable, test_c4_floor_tile_glyph_and_walkable, test_c4_tile_kinds_enum_has_required_kinds — all PASSED. Direct: tiles.wall().glyph='#', walkable=False; tiles.floor().glyph='.', walkable=True. TileKind enum exposes WALL/FLOOR/DOOR/UPSTAIRS/DOWNSTAIRS.

criteria.description: Floor with width/height and bounds-checked tile access
criteria.evidence: tests/test_sprint01.py::test_c5_floor_dimensions_and_in_bounds PASSED. Floor(20,10).width==20 and .height==10. in_bounds(-1,0)=False, (20,0)=False, (0,10)=False. Floor.get(-1,0) raises IndexError.

criteria.description: Entity base + Player subclass with (x,y) and '@' glyph
criteria.evidence: tests/test_sprint01.py::test_c6_entity_base_class_exists, test_c6_player_position_and_glyph — PASSED. issubclass(Player, Entity)=True. Player(5,7).x==5, .y==7, .glyph=='@'.

criteria.description: Game ties floor+player; constructible from seed
criteria.evidence: tests/test_sprint01.py::test_c7_game_from_seed_factory, test_c7_game_via_world_constructor_also_works — PASSED. Game.from_seed(seed=1) returns a Game with player at (22,35), in floor.in_bounds. Game(World(master_seed=1)) also works.

criteria.description: render_floor produces height x width ASCII string
criteria.evidence: tests/test_sprint01.py::test_c8_render_floor_dimensions_match PASSED. render_floor(Floor(10,5)).split('\n') -> 5 lines, each exactly 10 chars.

criteria.description: Frame renderer overlays '@' at player coords; exactly once
criteria.evidence: tests/test_sprint01.py::test_c9_frame_renderer_places_player_glyph_exactly_once PASSED. frame.split('\n')[py][px]=='@' and frame.count('@')==1 verified against Game.from_seed(1).

criteria.description: Frame uses documented glyph legend only; '#' and '@' present
criteria.evidence: tests/test_sprint01.py::test_c10_frame_uses_documented_glyph_set_only PASSED. Unique glyphs in frame: {'#','.','+','>','@'} — subset of {'#','.','+','<','>','@'}. '#' and '@' both present.

criteria.description: Player moves in all 8 directions onto walkable tiles
criteria.evidence: tests/test_sprint01.py::test_c11_eight_directional_movement_on_open_floor PASSED. From the center of an 11x11 open arena, all 8 (dx,dy) deltas — N/NE/E/SE/S/SW/W/NW — succeed and update position by exactly the requested offset.

criteria.description: Wall bump is no-op
criteria.evidence: tests/test_sprint01.py::test_c12_wall_bump_is_noop PASSED. On a 3x3 arena with one floor at (1,1), try_move(1,0) into wall returns False, position unchanged. Also covered by tests/test_game.py::test_player_cannot_walk_through_walls.

criteria.description: Out-of-bounds moves are no-ops, no raise
criteria.evidence: tests/test_sprint01.py::test_c13_oob_moves_are_noops_no_raise PASSED. On a 5x5 fully-walkable floor with player at (0,0): try_move(-1,0) -> False, try_move(0,-1) -> False, no exception, position unchanged. Game.try_move checks Floor.in_bounds before any tile access.

criteria.description: Turn counter increments on player actions
criteria.evidence: tests/test_sprint01.py::test_c14_turn_counter_advances_on_actions PASSED. Game.turns starts at 0; advances >=1 on a valid move and >=1 on a wall bump. Game.try_move increments self.turns on every code path.

criteria.description: --headless prints frame, exits 0
criteria.evidence: tests/test_sprint01.py::test_c15_headless_run_prints_frame_and_exits_zero PASSED. Manual: `python -m whisperdeep --headless --seed 1` exits 0, stdout 40 lines x 81 chars, contains '@' (count=1) and '#' (count=40 lines starting with walls).

criteria.description: --seed accepted; same-seed runs byte-identical
criteria.evidence: tests/test_sprint01.py::test_c16_same_seed_byte_identical_headless_output PASSED. Manual: two `python -m whisperdeep --headless --seed 1` runs produce byte-identical stdout (diff -q reports no difference).

criteria.description: tests/ exists; pytest exit 0; >=8 tests collected
criteria.evidence: `pytest -q` collected and passed 44 tests. tests/ contains test_generator.py, test_game.py, test_sprint01.py. Threshold required >=8.

criteria.description: Tests cover tile walkability, floor dims, movement, wall bump
criteria.evidence: tile walkability: test_c4_wall_tile_glyph_and_walkable + test_c4_floor_tile_glyph_and_walkable + test_c18_tile_walkability_wall_vs_floor (explicit wall vs floor). Floor dims: test_c5_floor_dimensions_and_in_bounds + test_generate_returns_floor_with_requested_dims. Movement: test_c11_eight_directional_movement_on_open_floor + test_player_can_walk_through_doors + test_game_launches_and_accepts_a_move. Wall bump: test_c12_wall_bump_is_noop + test_player_cannot_walk_through_walls.

criteria.description: README/docs covers run command, glyph legend, --seed/--headless
criteria.evidence: docs/whisperdeep.md contains: 'Quick start' section with `python -m whisperdeep --seed 1`; full glyph legend table listing #/./+/<>/@; 'Command-line flags' table documenting --seed and --headless. grep confirms all three topics present.

criteria.description: Zero third-party runtime dependencies
criteria.evidence: tests/test_sprint01.py::test_c20_no_third_party_runtime_imports PASSED. No pyproject.toml/requirements.txt declaring dependencies. Code only imports stdlib: argparse, dataclasses, enum, random, sys, typing, hashlib (test only), collections (test only). Heuristic guard checks every whisperdeep submodule against a denylist (numpy, tcod, rich, click, requests, ...) and finds no third-party imports.

criteria.description: Headless run: exit 0, no stderr traceback
criteria.evidence: tests/test_sprint01.py::test_c21_headless_subprocess_exit_zero_no_stderr_traceback PASSED. Manual subprocess.run([python, '-m', 'whisperdeep', '--headless', '--seed', '1']) -> returncode=0, stderr is 0 bytes, no 'Traceback' string.

recommendations: An independent evaluator with a fresh context should re-verify this report (per the sprint-02 precedent and the harness's structural-separation principle).
The interactive run loop in cli.run_interactive is intentionally minimal and uses input(); a real TUI is deferred to a later sprint per the contract's out-of-scope list.
